mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in non-farm payrolls (PAYEMS) indicates a strengthening economic recovery, leading to upward revisions in corporate earnings forecasts and driving positive equity returns over the medium term.
evidence_refs: fact:Labor market is a key input for GDP growth and consumer spending.
failure_condition: A positive 6-month change in PAYEMS fails to predict positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of instances over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: In a Zero Interest Rate Policy (ZIRP) environment, an unexpected acceleration in inflation (CPIAUCSL) will force the market to price in earlier-than-expected monetary tightening, increasing the discount rate and causing negative equity returns.
evidence_refs: fact:The Federal Reserve was engaged in unconventional monetary policy (QE) post-GFC.
failure_condition: S&P 500 returns are positive in the 20 days following an inflation print that meets the trigger condition in a majority of cases.

mechanism_narrative: A concurrent decline in both retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) signals a broad-based weakening of consumer and investment demand, increasing the probability of a double-dip recession and negatively impacting equity valuations.
candidate_trigger: 3-month change in RSAFS is negative AND 3-month change in HOUST is negative
evidence_refs: fact:Fears of a 'double-dip' recession were prevalent in the 2010-2011 period.
failure_condition: The S&P 500 posts positive 60-day returns in more than 60% of instances where both series show a 3-month decline.

mechanism_narrative: When starting from an elevated level, a significant drop in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) signals that the labor market recovery has strong momentum, boosting investor confidence and leading to positive short-term equity returns.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE > 7.0% and has fallen by at least 0.2pp over the prior 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:Unemployment was 9.6% as of the latest reading, well above historical norms.
failure_condition: The signal's hit rate (predicting positive 20d returns) is not statistically different from a random coin flip (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: As a high-frequency measure of economic output, accelerating industrial production (INDPRO) provides a real-time signal of strengthening business conditions, which positively correlates with near-term equity market performance.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO showed a strong V-shaped recovery after the GFC trough.
failure_condition: The rank information coefficient between the 1-month change in INDPRO and 5-day forward S&P 500 returns is less than 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp, negative surprise in monthly retail sales (RSAFS) indicates an abrupt slowdown in consumer spending, which constitutes the largest part of the US economy, triggering immediate negative revisions to growth forecasts and equity prices.
evidence_refs: fact:Consumer spending is approximately two-thirds of US GDP.
failure_condition: Instances of large negative retail sales prints are followed by positive 5-day returns more than 50% of the time, indicating a 'bad news is good news' Fed reaction dominates.

mechanism_narrative: With the policy rate at the zero-lower bound, rising inflation lowers real interest rates. This decline in real rates reduces the discount rate for future earnings and makes equities more attractive relative to bonds, driving positive returns.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS < 0.25% and 3-month change in CPIAUCSL is positive
evidence_refs: fact:Real yields were negative at this time point.
failure_condition: During ZIRP, periods of rising inflation are correlated with negative 20-day S&P 500 returns, suggesting inflation fears outweigh the real rate effect.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of high but falling unemployment (UNRATE) and low, stable inflation (CPIAUCSL) creates a 'Goldilocks' environment. This signals economic growth without triggering imminent monetary tightening, supporting higher equity multiples.
evidence_refs: fact:This reflects the Fed's dual mandate being in a state that allows for prolonged accommodation.
failure_condition: The average 60-day forward return when the trigger conditions are met is not statistically significantly greater than the unconditional average return.
